game_name: Fire Emblem: Shadow Dragon and the Blade of Light

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Marth begins with basic knight units and foundational grid-based movement
Players progressively recruit from 52 total recruitable characters across campaign
Mounted units, flying units, archers, mages, and healers each introduced with distinct range and movement capabilities
Weapons break after limited use; players must manage inventory and purchase replacements from towns
Core mechanic: defeated units are permanently removed from subsequent missions and storyline
NPCs in towns provide healing items, weapons, and story context; villages can be visited during missions

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters and key unit elimination
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Missions won by defeating commanders and key enemy units rather than eliminating all enemies
Female mages and male curates provide offensive and healing magic; staffs unavailable to female mages when unpromoted
Manakete dragon units appear; boss dragons (Morzas, Mannu) introduce specialized enemy types
Promotion items allow units to advance to stronger classes with improved stats and weapon access

element_introduction_order.element: Sacred treasures: Falchion and Fire Emblem
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Narrative-critical items required to defeat final boss Medeus; must be recovered from enemy hands
Optional arena battles provide currency outside scripted events; alternative income source for purchasing equipment

typical_relief_triggers: Successful recruitment of new allied units
Acquisition of healing items and weapons from towns
Passage of narrative chapters advancing the overall campaign
Defeat of boss characters and completion of map objectives
typical_tension_triggers: Permanent death of recruited units in battle
Exposure of Marth to enemy forces (game-ending if defeated)
Betrayals and reversals in narrative (e.g., Gra's betrayal of Altea)
Introduction of new enemy types with unfamiliar mechanics
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Linear story-driven progression with 25 chapters creating escalating difficulty; permanent death mechanic creates persistent tension and consequences; turn-based grid combat allows player-controlled pacing within individual battles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Towns and villages accessible during missions serve as optional waypoints for resource acquisition and NPC interaction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic mechanics with small army of trusted retainers; foundational sword combat and movement on simple grid layouts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expansion to multiple character classes (mounted, flying, magic); introduction of resource management and permanent death consequences; recruitment of diverse unit types
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dragon units and specialized enemy types; need for promoted high-level units; strategic resource management becomes critical; acquisition of sacred treasures (Falchion, Fire Emblem) required for final battles
notable_spikes: Chapter transitions introducing new enemy classes or boss types
Encounters with named boss characters (Mannu, Morzas) and their specialized units
Final chapters requiring possession of sacred treasures and high unit levels
Campaign climax against Shadow Dragon Medeus

notable_level_patterns.description: Chapters structured around recruiting specific allied units by either defeating them in combat or talking to them through designated recruitment conversations; units become permanently available after recruitment
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early chapters establish pattern; Sabre remains unused but programmed for recruitment by Ogma

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-battle pauses where players can visit towns or villages to purchase weapons, healing items, and interact with NPCs; provides resource replenishment and narrative context
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Recurring throughout campaign whenever towns are present on map

notable_level_patterns.description: Missions won by defeating key enemy commanders and boss characters rather than clearing entire map; allows for strategic retreat and tactical focus on priority targets
25-chapter linear progression advancing Marth's journey from exile through kingdom reclamation; each chapter follows story beats from recruitment through final confrontation with Medeus
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entire game structure; opening with exile on Talys island, ending with Shadow Dragon confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential recruitment of allied kingdoms (Aurelis, Archanea) with their leaders and retainers joining army; creates growing roster of available units with varying class specializations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Structured across early-to-mid game chapters as Marth travels across Archanea
